## [00:00 - 01:31]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about whether remarketing on Facebook is still necessary. Let's go. Is remarketing on Facebook dead? So this has been a popular claim lately. I will agree that certain types of remarketing may be dead. So you can go broad and as long as you optimize for a conversion event, you often end up reaching the right people anyway and many of these people would have been in your remarketing audiences. So when we're talking about general remarketing with the goal of reaching a warmer audience of people who are more likely to convert, yeah that might not be quite as necessary anymore. But we're missing something that's pretty important. Primary example of this, abandoned cart. An abandoned cart situation allows you to show a unique message to this group of people to say, hey did you forget something? Here's the discount. Complete that purchase. This message is obviously going to make no sense to people outside of that group. So is abandoned shopping carts still effective? Heck yeah. Is it time to completely abandon that type of remarketing? Of course not. So I hope we're on the same page on this. Let me know what you think. Follow for more. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok.
